data.title: Skillsoft Earnings Call: AI Pivot Lifts Margins
data.description:
Skillsoft Corp ((SKIL)) has held its Q4 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Skillsoft’s latest earnings call painted a cautiously optimistic picture, with management stressing tangible progress on its AI-led transformation, tighter cost control and stronger cash generation. While revenue slipped and GAAP losses widened on impairments and restructuring, the tone leaned more constructive as margin gains and surging AI engagement suggested the turnaround is taking hold despite macro and geopolitical headwinds.

AI-Native Pivot Recasts Skillsoft’s Core Business

Skillsoft declared its strategic overhaul largely complete, repositioning itself as an AI-native skills platform built around content, platform and data. A centerpiece is the new AI-native Percipio platform, unveiled in September and moving to general availability in February, which management views as the foundation for future growth and competitive differentiation.

Cost Cuts Fuel Targeted Reinvestment

The company removed roughly $45 million of gross costs, then deliberately recycled about half into go-to-market resources and AI product innovation. Executives emphasized that this reflects a more disciplined capital allocation approach, trimming legacy spending while still funding the areas they see as highest return in the new strategy.

New Platform Shows Early Customer Traction

On the product side, Skillsoft advanced its roadmap, including upgrades to its CAISY AI capabilities and the broad rollout of the new platform. The offering already has 15 paying customers, and the company is using the platform internally to refine features and accelerate learning, signaling early but tangible traction in the market.

AI Engagement Metrics Surge Across the Portfolio

Management highlighted sharp year-over-year increases in AI usage, pointing to strong adoption of its newer capabilities. AI skill benchmark completions jumped 994% while AI content and AI Journey completions rose 261% and 222% respectively, and CAISY users and launches climbed triple digits, underpinning confidence in AI as a key growth driver.

Margins Improve on Stronger Adjusted EBITDA

Adjusted EBITDA in the quarter reached $31.2 million, up about 4% from a year earlier, and the adjusted EBITDA margin widened to 23.9% from 22.4%. The TDS segment contributed roughly $33 million of EBITDA and drove most of the improvement, underscoring the segment’s role as the company’s profit engine.

Free Cash Flow Turns Positive and Leverage Eases

Free cash flow more than doubled year over year in the quarter to $26.5 million, helping full-year free cash flow turn modestly positive at around $6.5 million, slightly above expectations. Cash and equivalents stood at $104.5 million while net debt edged down to roughly $474 million, signaling incremental balance sheet progress.

Expense Efficiency Supports Growth Investments

Operating expenses fell meaningfully, with content and software development and selling and marketing each down around 5% and G&A lower by about 13% year over year. Total operating expenses declined 4.2%, or $4.3 million, enabling Skillsoft to reinvest savings into strategic growth areas without sacrificing profitability.

Guidance Highlights Profitable TDS Outlook

Looking ahead, Skillsoft guided TDS fiscal 2027 revenue to $388 million–$406 million and adjusted EBITDA to $108 million–$116 million, implying margins around 28%. The company also forecast TDS free cash flow of $14 million–$22 million, excluding Global Knowledge, signaling confidence in the segment’s cash generation despite modest top-line expectations.

Revenue Declines and Mixed Top-Line Trends

Total revenue for the quarter was $130.7 million, down 2.3% year over year, as growth remained elusive. TDS revenue of $102.6 million was essentially flat, while the Learner and Global Knowledge businesses failed to expand, reflecting lingering demand challenges in certain parts of the portfolio.

Global Knowledge Drags, Triggering Strategic Review

Global Knowledge revenue fell about 9.4% to $28 million, continuing its underperformance relative to the rest of the business. Management has launched a strategic review and potential divestiture of the unit, citing its weaker growth profile and a desire to focus capital and management attention on higher-priority assets.

GAAP Loss Widens as Adjusted Earnings Slip

GAAP net loss deepened to $36.7 million for the quarter, compared with $31.1 million a year ago, largely due to an intangible impairment and higher restructuring costs. Adjusted net income also retreated to $11 million, with adjusted EPS sliding from $2.11 to $1.26, highlighting the gap between underlying profitability and reported results.

Retention Solid but Upgrades Under Pressure

Skillsoft reported a TDS trailing twelve-month dollar retention rate of 98%, down from 105% in the prior-year period, as upgrade activity cooled. While customer retention improved, the decline in upsell reflected tougher comparisons and a cautious spending environment, limiting expansion revenue for now.

Higher Utilization Lifts Costs of Revenue

Cost of revenue increased 2.5% year over year to $34.2 million, or 26% of revenue, driven by higher labs and certification spending tied to greater customer utilization. Management is adjusting agreement structures to curb future cost overruns, suggesting some of the pressure is being addressed contractually.

Macro and Geopolitics Weigh on Bookings and Sales

Executives cited executive orders, government market disruptions and broader geopolitical instability as factors damping bookings in the quarter. They also noted that the Global Knowledge sale process has been hampered by conflict in the Middle East and limited buyer availability, adding uncertainty around the timing and outcome of any transaction.

Conservative Outlook Reflects Ongoing Consumer Weakness

The midpoint of TDS guidance implies a slight year-over-year revenue decline of about $7 million, driven almost entirely by the consumer-focused learner business, which represents roughly 9% of TDS. Management signaled that this segment is expected to continue shrinking, and that the overall outlook intentionally embeds a conservative stance amid persistent macro risks.

In sum, Skillsoft’s earnings call showcased a company leaning into AI and efficiency to offset revenue softness and macro uncertainty. Investors will be watching to see whether strong AI adoption, margin expansion and cash generation can eventually translate into more durable top-line growth, especially as management reshapes the portfolio and executes on its TDS-focused strategy.

data.title: Direct Digital Holdings Earnings Call Highlights Pivot
data.description:
Direct Digital Holdings, Inc. Class A ((DRCT)) has held its Q4 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Direct Digital Holdings, Inc. struck a cautious tone on its latest earnings call, balancing clear operational progress with mounting financial strain. Management highlighted strong buy-side momentum, deep cost cuts, and a new AI-driven product, yet these positives were overshadowed by a collapse in sell-side revenue, weaker margins, a larger net loss, tightening liquidity, and a Nasdaq listing deficiency that clouds near-term visibility.

Buy-Side Revenue Acceleration

Buy-side activity remained the brightest spot, with Q4 2025 buy-side revenue up about 28% year over year to $8.2 million from $6.4 million. For full-year 2025, buy-side revenue grew roughly 10%, powered by new and expanded relationships across travel and tourism, higher education, and energy, underscoring the platform’s continued relevance to advertisers.

Full-Year Sales and New Customer Wins

Consolidated sales for 2025 totaled $34.7 million, reflecting a business under pressure but still adding new clients. New verticals and customers contributed about $1.7 million in Q4 and roughly $7 million for the full year, signaling that diversification efforts are gaining traction even as legacy lines shrink.

Operating Expense Reductions

Management leaned heavily on cost discipline, cutting operating expenses 12% in Q4 2025 to $6.7 million from $7.7 million in the prior-year quarter. For the full year, opex fell 18% to $25.2 million, a $5.4 million reduction versus 2024, with an additional expected $0.5 million in quarterly savings starting in Q2 2026 as remaining sell-side contracts wind down.

New Product Launch — Ignition+

In March 2026, the company introduced Ignition+, an AI-enabled programmatic media solution aimed at enterprise and mid-market buy-side clients. The product is designed to improve transparency and efficiency while centralizing media buying, and management is positioning it as a key growth lever to capture more demand-side wallet share.

Capital Structure Actions and Access to Capital

To shore up its balance sheet, Direct Digital converted debt into $25 million of convertible preferred stock and issued an additional $10 million of Series A preferred in Q4 2025. The firm also expanded its equity reserve facility by 50 million shares, creating roughly $100 million of potential capacity and raising $7.3 million via that facility during 2025, underlining reliance on external capital.

Strategic Realignment and Guidance

The company is restructuring around a single reporting segment in 2026, sharpening its focus on the buy side and phasing out underperforming sell-side exposure. Management reiterated its goal to return to positive platform growth and to reach breakeven or better on a quarterly basis by the second half of 2026, targeting about 10% year-over-year growth on an annualized basis.

Sell-Side Revenue Collapse

The most dramatic weak spot was sell-side revenue, which plunged to just $0.2 million in Q4 2025 from $2.7 million a year earlier, a decline of about 92.6%. This drop, driven largely by lower impression inventory, confirms a material contraction in the sell-side business and validates the strategic pivot toward buy-side activities.

Decline in Revenue and Margin Pressure

Despite buy-side gains, overall Q4 2025 revenue slipped around 7.7% to $8.4 million from $9.1 million in the prior-year quarter. Gross margin also compressed, dropping to 27% from 32% a year earlier, as the mix shift and operational headwinds weighed on profitability and limited the benefits of cost reductions.

Worsening Net Loss and EBITDA Performance

The bottom line deteriorated sharply, with Q4 2025 net loss widening to $12.6 million from $6.6 million, largely due to approximately $7.4 million in non-operational financing costs. Adjusted EBITDA loss edged slightly higher to $3.6 million from $3.4 million, while operating loss was flat at $4.5 million, indicating that underlying operations have stabilized but remain firmly in negative territory.

Liquidity Shortfall and Working Capital Strain

Liquidity emerged as a clear concern, with cash and cash equivalents ending 2025 at just $700,000, down from $1.4 million a year earlier. Total cash plus accounts receivable fell to $3.9 million from $6.4 million, a roughly 39% decline that underscores tighter working capital and limits flexibility to absorb further shocks without additional financing.

Nasdaq Listing Deficiency and Governance Actions

The company disclosed a Nasdaq listing deficiency tied to stockholders’ equity, heightening regulatory and investor-confidence risks around its shares. In response, the board approved a 55-for-1 reverse stock split effective early January 2026 and is working with advisers on remediation steps, signaling urgency to preserve market listing and access to public capital.

Financing Costs and One-Time Charges

Heavy financing-related expenses weighed on Q4 results, with around $7.4 million in non-operational costs materially inflating the net loss. The continued use of preferred stock issuance and equity facilities as primary funding tools points to an ongoing dependence on capital markets to stabilize the balance sheet while the operating model resets.

Forward-Looking Outlook and Strategic Priorities

Looking ahead, management is banking on a buy-side-led recovery, expecting positive platform growth and breakeven or better quarterly performance by the back half of 2026 and guiding to roughly 10% annualized revenue growth. The plan hinges on Ignition+, further cost savings from sell-side wind-downs, disciplined spending, and both organic and inorganic demand-side expansion, all against a backdrop of thin liquidity and capital-structure repair.

Direct Digital’s latest earnings call framed a company in transition, with encouraging buy-side traction and cost control offset by severe sell-side erosion and financial strain. For investors, the story now centers on whether management can execute its pivot, repair the balance sheet, and cure listing issues fast enough to translate operational momentum into sustainable growth and profitability.

data.title: Red Mountain to Restart U.S. Antimony Exploration as Critical Minerals Support Grows
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Red Mountain Mining Ltd ( (AU:RMX) ) is now available.

Red Mountain Mining plans to restart exploration at its U.S. antimony portfolio as winter conditions ease, beginning with systematic geological mapping and surface sampling at the Thompson Falls project on the Montana-Idaho border. Initial rock chip work at the historical Eastern Star Mine has already delivered high-grade antimony and detectable gold, and the company aims to define drill targets by tracing the mineralised vein system and sampling other historical workings across the claims.

The company has strengthened its technical capability by appointing experienced geologist Dr Max Baker as an advisor and engaging Montana-based KC Harvey Environmental to help expedite environmental approvals for rapid progression to drilling and resource definition. Red Mountain also intends to extend mapping and sampling to its Yellow Pine and Silver Dollar projects in Idaho, as U.S. government backing for critical minerals, including nearby antimony developments, underpins the strategic importance of its portfolio and may open avenues for non-dilutive funding support.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:RMX) stock is a Sell with a A$0.02 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Red Mountain Mining Ltd stock, see the AU:RMX Stock Forecast page.

More about Red Mountain Mining Ltd

Red Mountain Mining Ltd is an ASX- and US OTCQB-listed critical minerals exploration and development company with projects in Tier-1 mining districts in the United States and Australia. The company is focused on antimony, a key critical metal, with a portfolio of projects in Montana and Idaho positioned near existing processing infrastructure and established mining operations.

data.title: Gold Mountain Doubles Potential Resource Footprint at Brazilian Rare Earths Prospect
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Gold Mountain Limited ( (AU:GMN) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Gold Mountain Limited has reported new diamond drilling assay results from the Irajuba IR-1 rare earths prospect in Brazil, with best intersections including 29 metres at 3,964 ppm total rare earth oxides and a higher-grade 17-metre section at 6,151 ppm. The latest drilling, concentrated south and west of the previously defined exploration target, intersected thick zones of high-grade mineralisation and extends the potential resource footprint.

As a result of this campaign, the area of potential resource at the Irajuba IR-1 prospect has effectively doubled from 2.1 to 4.3 square kilometres, significantly enhancing the scale potential of the project. This expansion underscores Gold Mountain’s growing rare earths footprint in Brazil and may strengthen its strategic position in the critical minerals sector as the company advances from exploration targets toward delineating more substantial resources.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:GMN) stock is a Hold with a A$0.10 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Gold Mountain Limited stock, see the AU:GMN Stock Forecast page.

More about Gold Mountain Limited

Gold Mountain Limited is an ASX-listed exploration company focused on critical minerals projects in Brazil and Papua New Guinea. Its portfolio spans lithium, copper and rare earth element (REE) prospects, with key Brazilian targets in the Cococi, Iguatu, Jequie and Serido belts, positioning the company to supply minerals essential for electric vehicles and advanced technologies.

data.title: Capital Group ceases to be substantial shareholder in Megaport
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Megaport ( (AU:MP1) ) has issued an update.

Megaport has disclosed that The Capital Group Companies and its related investment management entities have ceased to be substantial shareholders in the company as of 7 April 2026. The change follows on-market disposals of Megaport common stock by Capital Research and Management Company, which reduced the group’s voting power below the substantial holding threshold and signals a notable shift in the company’s institutional investor base.

The filing clarifies that Capital Group and its affiliates do not hold Megaport shares for their own account but manage them on behalf of client portfolios, meaning the exit primarily reflects portfolio management decisions for those clients. The reduction in a major institutional stake may affect perceptions of Megaport’s share register composition and could influence market sentiment around the stock, even though it does not directly alter the company’s operations or strategy.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:MP1) stock is a Hold with a A$7.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Megaport stock, see the AU:MP1 Stock Forecast page.

More about Megaport

Megaport Ltd. operates in the network connectivity and cloud services sector, providing elastic interconnection services that link enterprises to cloud providers, data centres, and network services globally. The company targets businesses seeking flexible, on-demand connectivity to support cloud adoption and digital transformation strategies.

data.title: Talent Infinity Showcases High-Grade Potential at Buster Antimony-Gold Project in B.C.
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Talent Infinity Resource Developments, Inc. ( (TSE:TICO) ) just unveiled an update.

Talent Infinity Resource Developments Inc. has outlined key geological and historical exploration highlights for its 1,092-hectare Buster Antimony-Gold Project in the Clinton Mining Division of British Columbia. The project hosts a structurally controlled epithermal gold-antimony system within early Cretaceous sedimentary rocks intruded by quartz-feldspar porphyry, with mineralization associated with fault zones and intrusive contacts.

Historical work at Buster has returned high-grade gold intercepts from diamond drilling and trench samples, alongside strong antimony and pathfinder element anomalies that define multiple target zones over a kilometre-scale footprint. Management views the acquisition as a strategic addition that broadens its critical minerals portfolio in a mining-friendly jurisdiction, potentially enhancing its growth pipeline, though it cautions that historic results have not yet been verified by its Qualified Person and may not be indicative of future outcomes.

Spark’s Take on TICO Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TICO is a Underperform.

TSE:TICO currently faces significant financial and operational challenges, with zero revenue, substantial losses, and high financial leverage. Technical indicators are weak, and the lack of valuation metrics adds to the risk profile. However, recent corporate events, including executive changes and strategic advisories, provide a glimmer of potential improvement. Overall, the stock is high-risk with limited immediate upside potential.

To see Spark’s full report on TICO stock, click here.

More about Talent Infinity Resource Developments, Inc.

Talent Infinity Resource Developments Inc. is a mineral exploration company focused on acquiring, exploring and developing critical mineral properties in mining-friendly jurisdictions. The company is listed on the CSE under the symbol TICO and on the FSE under the symbol 0N8, targeting assets with exposure to key strategic metals such as antimony and gold.
